name: Powell Street Cable Car
why_special: The most iconic transportation experience in San Francisco.
description: San Francisco’s historic cable cars climb the steep hills of the city while offering views of the bay and historic neighborhoods.
tip: Stand on the side rail for the classic San Francisco experience.

name: Lombard Street
why_special: Often called the crookedest street in the world.
description: Eight sharp hairpin turns wind down a steep hill lined with flowers and charming houses.
tip: Walk down from the top for the best views.

name: Fisherman's Wharf
why_special: San Francisco’s lively historic waterfront district.
description: Fisherman’s Wharf offers seafood restaurants, historic piers and views of Alcatraz Island.
tip: The sea lions at Pier 39 are a must-see.

name: Pier 39
why_special: A lively pier famous for sea lions and bay views.
description: Pier 39 combines restaurants, shops, street performers and panoramic bay views.
tip: Visit the sea lion docks for great photos.

name: Battery Spencer
why_special: The most dramatic viewpoint of the Golden Gate Bridge.
description: Located in the Marin Headlands, Battery Spencer overlooks the Golden Gate Bridge with the San Francisco skyline behind it.
tip: Arrive before sunset for golden light.

name: Tunnel View
why_special: The most famous viewpoint in Yosemite Valley.
description: Tunnel View reveals Yosemite Valley’s iconic panorama including El Capitan, Half Dome and Bridalveil Fall.
tip: This is the classic Yosemite postcard viewpoint.

name: Yosemite Valley
why_special: The dramatic heart of Yosemite National Park.
description: Yosemite Valley is surrounded by towering granite cliffs including El Capitan and Half Dome.
tip: Take time to simply walk and enjoy the valley views.

name: Yosemite Falls
why_special: One of the tallest waterfalls in North America.
description: Yosemite Falls plunges dramatically down granite cliffs and is especially powerful during spring snowmelt.
tip: Visit during May for peak waterfall flow.

name: Bridalveil Fall
why_special: One of Yosemite’s most beautiful waterfalls.
description: Bridalveil Fall plunges nearly 188 meters into Yosemite Valley creating mist and rainbows.
tip: Expect mist near the base of the falls.

name: El Capitan
why_special: One of the world’s most famous rock climbing destinations.
description: El Capitan is a massive vertical granite monolith rising over 900 meters from Yosemite Valley.
tip: Look carefully — climbers can sometimes be seen halfway up the wall.

name: Hollywood Walk of Fame
why_special: The most famous sidewalk in the entertainment world.
description: More than 2,700 stars embedded in the sidewalks honor actors, musicians and filmmakers.
tip: Look for the stars of your favorite movie characters.

name: Dolby Theatre
why_special: Home of the Academy Awards.
description: The Dolby Theatre hosts the Oscars ceremony and major Hollywood premieres.
tip: The plaza offers a distant view of the Hollywood sign.

name: Lake Hollywood Park
why_special: One of the closest public viewpoints of the Hollywood sign.
description: This quiet park provides an unobstructed view of the Hollywood sign above the hills.
tip: Great photo spot without the crowds.

name: Griffith Observatory
why_special: The best panoramic viewpoint in Los Angeles.
description: The observatory overlooks the entire Los Angeles basin, the Hollywood sign and the Pacific Ocean.
tip: Sunset is the most beautiful time to visit.

name: Disneyland Park
why_special: The original Disney theme park created by Walt Disney.
description: Disneyland combines imaginative rides, themed lands and beloved characters into a magical experience.
tip: Start early in Fantasyland for the shortest lines.

name: Las Vegas Strip
why_special: The most famous entertainment boulevard in the world.
description: The Las Vegas Strip is lined with extravagant resorts and casinos that recreate global landmarks.
tip: Walk the strip after sunset when the lights are most spectacular.

name: Bellagio Fountains
why_special: One of the most famous fountain shows in the world.
description: The Bellagio fountains perform choreographed water shows set to music with jets reaching incredible heights.
tip: Shows run every 15–30 minutes in the evening.

name: High Roller Observation Wheel
why_special: The tallest observation wheel in North America.
description: The High Roller provides panoramic views of the Las Vegas skyline from glass observation pods.
tip: Night rides provide the best view of the illuminated Strip.

name: Fly LINQ Zipline
why_special: A thrilling zipline ride above the LINQ promenade.
description: Fly LINQ sends riders soaring above the promenade toward the High Roller observation wheel.
tip: Try the superhero flying position for the best experience.

name: Las Vegas Strip Helicopter Night Flight
why_special: The most spectacular way to see the Las Vegas skyline.
description: This helicopter ride flies above the illuminated Las Vegas Strip revealing the glowing resorts and landmarks from the air.
tip: Choose a night flight for the best views.

name: Fremont Street Experience
why_special: The historic heart of Las Vegas with a massive LED canopy light show.
description: Fremont Street features one of the world’s largest LED screens stretching above the street.
tip: Stay for the hourly light shows.

name: Las Vegas Sphere
why_special: The world’s largest spherical entertainment venue.
description: The Sphere lights up with animated LED displays visible across the Las Vegas skyline.
tip: Best viewed from the Strip at night.

name: Niagara Falls
why_special: One of the most powerful waterfalls in the world.
description: Niagara Falls is a dramatic natural spectacle where massive curtains of water plunge into the Niagara Gorge.
tip: Visit during both daylight and night illumination.

name: Maid of the Mist
why_special: The closest boat experience to the falls.
description: The Maid of the Mist boat ride takes visitors directly into the mist of the waterfalls.
tip: Expect to get wet despite the poncho.

name: Cave of the Winds
why_special: A thrilling walkway experience beside the falls.
description: Visitors walk along wooden platforms near Bridal Veil Falls where powerful water sprays surround the path.
tip: Wear the provided sandals and poncho.

name: White House
why_special: Residence of the President of the United States.
description: The White House has served as the residence of every U.S. president since 1800.
tip: The best public view is from Lafayette Square.

name: Washington Monument
why_special: The towering obelisk honoring George Washington.
description: Standing at the center of the National Mall, the Washington Monument is one of the most recognizable landmarks in America.
tip: The Reflecting Pool offers iconic views.

name: Lincoln Memorial
why_special: One of the most powerful monuments in the United States.
description: The Lincoln Memorial honors Abraham Lincoln and overlooks the Reflecting Pool.
tip: Visit near sunset for beautiful lighting.

name: United States Capitol
why_special: The seat of the United States Congress.
description: The Capitol building is one of the most recognizable government buildings in the world.
tip: The east lawn offers the best photos.

name: SUMMIT One Vanderbilt
why_special: An immersive observation deck that transforms the skyline into a mirrored art installation.
description: SUMMIT One Vanderbilt combines architecture, digital art and breathtaking skyline views.
tip: Try to book a time that allows both daylight and sunset views.

name: Grand Central Terminal
why_special: One of the most beautiful railway stations ever built.
description: Opened in 1913, Grand Central Terminal is famous for its celestial ceiling mural.
tip: Look up in the main hall to see the constellation ceiling.

name: Bryant Park
why_special: A charming green oasis surrounded by Midtown skyscrapers.
description: Bryant Park sits behind the New York Public Library and offers lawns and cafes.
tip: Grab a coffee and enjoy the skyline views.

name: Top of the Rock
why_special: One of the best panoramic skyline views in New York.
description: Top of the Rock offers unobstructed views across Manhattan including Central Park.
tip: Arrive about 60 minutes before sunset.

name: Rockefeller Center Plaza
why_special: A historic complex famous for the Christmas tree and ice skating rink.
description: Rockefeller Center is an Art Deco landmark that houses NBC studios and restaurants.
tip: Visit at night when the plaza lights create a vibrant atmosphere.

name: Times Square
why_special: The most famous illuminated intersection in the world.
description: Times Square is known for massive digital billboards and Broadway theaters.
tip: Visit after dark to experience the full spectacle of lights.

name: Battery Park
why_special: The southern gateway of Manhattan with harbor views.
description: Battery Park offers waterfront promenades and serves as the departure point for Statue of Liberty ferries.
tip: Arrive early before your ferry departure.

name: Statue of Liberty
why_special: The most iconic symbol of freedom in the United States.
description: Standing at the entrance to New York Harbor, the Statue of Liberty welcomed millions of immigrants.
tip: Take the earliest ferry of the day to avoid long queues.

name: Ellis Island
why_special: The historic immigration gateway of America.
description: Ellis Island processed more than 12 million immigrants entering the United States.
tip: Explore the immigration museum.

name: Wall Street
why_special: The financial heart of the United States.
description: Wall Street is home to the New York Stock Exchange and historic financial institutions.
tip: Take a photo with the Charging Bull sculpture nearby.

name: Brooklyn Bridge
why_special: One of the most famous bridges in the world.
description: Completed in 1883, the Brooklyn Bridge connects Manhattan and Brooklyn.
tip: Walk from Manhattan to Brooklyn for the best views.

name: DUMBO
why_special: One of the most photogenic neighborhoods in New York.
description: DUMBO is known for cobblestone streets and iconic skyline views.
tip: Visit Washington Street for the famous Manhattan Bridge photo.

name: Brooklyn Bridge Park
why_special: The best waterfront skyline view of Manhattan.
description: Brooklyn Bridge Park stretches along the East River offering dramatic skyline views.
tip: Sunset here creates spectacular photos.

name: Intrepid Sea Air & Space Museum
why_special: A real aircraft carrier converted into a museum.
description: The museum includes the USS Intrepid aircraft carrier and the Space Shuttle Enterprise.
tip: Head to the Space Shuttle pavilion early.

name: Circle Line Sightseeing Cruise
why_special: The best way to see Manhattan’s skyline from the water.
description: The cruise circles Manhattan offering views of the Statue of Liberty and bridges.
tip: Sit on the upper deck for unobstructed views.

name: The High Line
why_special: A park built on an elevated historic railway line.
description: The High Line blends gardens, public art and skyline views along Manhattan’s west side.
tip: Walk southbound for the best city reveal.

name: Hudson Yards
why_special: New York’s newest futuristic skyline district.
description: Hudson Yards features modern skyscrapers, luxury shopping and the Vessel structure.
tip: Great place for photos after walking the High Line.

name: Friends Apartment Building
why_special: Exterior building used in the TV series Friends.
description: Located in Greenwich Village, this building served as the exterior establishing shot for Monica’s apartment.
tip: Combine with a visit to nearby Washington Square Park.

name: Washington Square Park
why_special: The cultural heart of Greenwich Village.
description: Washington Square Park features the iconic arch and lively street performances.
tip: Great place to experience NYC street culture.

name: Empire State Building
why_special: The most iconic skyscraper in New York.
description: Completed in 1931, the Empire State Building symbolizes the rise of New York.
tip: Visit late evening to avoid peak queues.

name: Central Park
why_special: A massive green oasis in the middle of Manhattan.
description: Central Park offers lakes, bridges and scenic walking paths across 843 acres.
tip: Bow Bridge and Bethesda Terrace are the most picturesque locations.

name: American Museum of Natural History
why_special: One of the world’s greatest natural history museums.
description: The museum contains exhibits ranging from dinosaurs to space exploration.
tip: Do not miss the giant blue whale exhibit.
